qa.overall_assessment: CLOSE: NO. Three independent blocking issues prevent close: (1) 4 Standard-3 venv violations on provably-new diff lines in commands/push.md and commands/dev.md; (2) 1 cleanliness finding with introduced_in_diff: true (stale README) forces CLOSE: NO per AC-2.6; (3) aggregate dev-report JSON is invalid (octal escape sequences in baseline_dirty_snapshot). All 5 workers individually passed QA. The blocking issues are in newly-introduced documentation lines and a corrupted aggregate artifact — not in the implementations themselves.

ac26_line_level_analysis.mechanism: AC-2.6(b) line-level rule: close.md independently verifies newness by checking (i) line falls within cycle diff's changed-line range/diff-hunk overlap AND (ii) finding is absent from pre-diff baseline
ac26_line_level_analysis.default_safe_rule_applicability: Default-safe rule for ambiguity (absent marker → ignore) applies to FILE-LEVEL inspectors (cleanliness, prompt). Line-level inspectors (style) have close.md do the diff check — the absence of introduced_in_diff on the violation object is NOT the ambiguity case; close.md performs its own verification.
ac26_line_level_analysis.conclusion: All 4 violations are provably new per AC-2.6(b) line-level mechanism. They force CLOSE: NO.
ac26_line_level_analysis.stdlib_exception_analysis: The push.md prose note at lines 208-210 ('Note: python3 is used directly (no venv activation needed) because the script uses only Python stdlib modules') is an explanatory comment, NOT a formal Standard 3 exception. Standard 3 in agents/style-inspector.md has no documented stdlib-only exception. Per Standard 3 severity rules: 'ALL violations are critical. There are no major or minor classifications.' The prose note cannot override the standard. This is a Standard 3 violation, not a legitimate exemption.

invalid_json_finding.error: Invalid JSON: baseline_dirty_snapshot field contains literal octal escape sequences \343\200\202 representing the CJK ideographic full stop character U+3002 (。) from filename .hook-refactor-allow。 in git status output. Python json.load() raises 'Invalid \escape: line 6 column 62'.
invalid_json_finding.impact: Downstream tools that parse the aggregate dev-report (score-update.sh, close.md verdict logic, future cycle provenance checks) will fail. This is a downstream consumability blocker.

cleanliness_finding_analysis.finding: tests/generated/20260524-125300-B/README.md stale auto-section (Total files: 1 vs actual 9 files)
cleanliness_finding_analysis.ac26_analysis: AC-2.6 verdict plumbing: any file-level finding tagged introduced_in_diff: true forces CLOSE: NO. There is no severity threshold in AC-2.6 — minor findings tagged introduced_in_diff: true are as blocking as critical. The cleanliness inspector's own 'non-blocking' summary is the inspector's assessment of the finding within the cleanliness domain; it does NOT override close.md's AC-2.6 verdict rule. Per AC-2.6, this finding forces CLOSE: NO.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 5 findings, all with PROPOSED_FIX. All 5 are blocking and consistent with CLOSE: NO verdict. Key findings: (1) Line-level Standard-3 findings ARE blocking — AC-2.6 absent-marker default-safe rule applies to file-level inspectors, not line-level; close.md independently verifies newness for style-inspector. (2) push.md stdlib note is an explanation, not a formal exception — Standard 3 has no stdlib carve-out. (3) dev.md violations are straightforward Standard-3 fixes. (4) Cleanliness stale README with introduced_in_diff: true forces CLOSE: NO per AC-2.6 — no severity threshold. (5) Invalid JSON in aggregate dev-report (octal escapes from CJK filename) is a downstream consumability blocker.

codex_findings.issue: Line-level Standard-3 findings at push.md:184, push.md:191, dev.md:709, dev.md:747 are blocking per AC-2.6 line-level rule — the absent introduced_in_diff marker on the violation object does not apply the default-safe rule here because close.md independently verifies newness for line-level inspectors
codex_findings.proposed_fix: CLOSE: NO. Fix: use 'source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python' for all 4 invocations, or formally amend Standard 3 to add a stdlib-only exception with explicit documentation. The prose note in push.md is not sufficient.

codex_findings.issue: push.md stdlib-only rationale is explanatory prose, not a formal Standard 3 exception. Standard 3 text in agents/style-inspector.md has no stdlib carve-out. The Bash(python3:*) allow entry in settings.json governs tool policy, not style standards.
codex_findings.proposed_fix: Either (a) replace python3 invocations with venv-activated variants, or (b) formally amend Standard 3 in agents/style-inspector.md to add: 'Exception: stdlib-only scripts (no third-party imports) may use python3 directly, documented in the invoking file.' The latter requires a separate cycle.
codex_findings.disposition: incorporated — confirms violation is not exempted

codex_findings.issue: dev.md violations at lines 709 and 747 are straightforward Standard-3 violations with no rationale offered. Simpler fix than push.md.
codex_findings.proposed_fix: Replace 'python3 scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id $TASK_ID' with 'source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id "$TASK_ID"' at both locations.

codex_findings.issue: Cleanliness stale README finding with introduced_in_diff: true forces CLOSE: NO per AC-2.6. The cleanliness inspector's 'non-blocking' verdict is its own domain assessment and does not override close.md's AC-2.6 rule which has no severity threshold.
codex_findings.proposed_fix: Regenerate the README via doc-sync hook (or manually update the AUTO section to reflect 9 files), then re-run cleanliness inspector to confirm the finding is resolved.
codex_findings.disposition: incorporated — confirms this is an additional CLOSE: NO trigger

codex_findings.issue: docs/dev/dev-report-20260524-125300.json (and -B variant) contain invalid JSON: baseline_dirty_snapshot field includes octal escape sequences \343\200\202 from git status output for the filename .hook-refactor-allow。 (CJK ideographic full stop U+3002). json.load() raises Invalid \escape.
codex_findings.proposed_fix: Repair the aggregate dev-report JSON: replace the raw octal/non-ASCII escape sequence with either the literal Unicode character or a sanitized filename representation. Downstream tools (score-update.sh, provenance checks) will fail on the current file.
codex_findings.disposition: incorporated — adds a third independent CLOSE: NO trigger

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 5 codex findings incorporated. Draft verdict shifted from tentative CLOSE: YES to confirmed CLOSE: NO based on codex's analysis of AC-2.6 line-level vs file-level distinction, Standard 3 scope, cleanliness AC-2.6 rule, and invalid JSON finding.

refined_context.recommended_approach: Targeted fixes only — no re-implementation needed. All 5 worker implementations are sound. Fix the 4 documentation lines in push.md and dev.md, repair the JSON serialization of baseline_dirty_snapshot in the aggregate dev-report generator, and regenerate the B-batch README. Consider formally amending Standard 3 in agents/style-inspector.md to add a stdlib-only exception if the architectural decision to use python3 directly for stdlib-only scripts is intentional.
refined_context.additional_context: The implementations from all 5 workers (A: bash context strip, B: Write guard sentinel, C: spec-continuation, D: dev provenance, push: atomic push script) are all correct and individually QA-passed. The blocking issues are purely in documentation lines and artifact serialization — not in the code changes themselves.
